43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	
95	\leanok
96	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
97	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
98	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
99	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
100	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
101	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
102	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
103	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
104	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
105	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
106	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
107	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
108	  % \varphi$."
109	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
110	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
111	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
112	  \[
113	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
114	  \]
115	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
116	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
117	  \[
118	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
119	  \]
120	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
121	
122	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
123	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
124	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
125	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
126	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
127	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
128	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
129	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
130	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
131	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
132	\end{definition}
133	
134	\begin{definition}
135	
136	
137	\leanok
138	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
139	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
140	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
141	  
142	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
143	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
144	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
145	  % PDF page 22).
146	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
147	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
148	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
149	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
150	  conclusion).}
151	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
152	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
153	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
154	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
155	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
156	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
157	
158	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
159	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
160	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
161	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
162	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
163	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
164	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
165	  is empty) as a single line.
166	
167	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
168	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
169	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
170	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
171	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
172	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
173	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
174	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
175	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
176	\end{definition}
177	
178	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
179	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
180	
181	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
182	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
183	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
184	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
185	
186	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
187	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
188	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
189	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
190	
191	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
192	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
193	
194	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
195	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
196	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
197	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
198	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
199	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
200	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
201	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
202	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
203	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
204	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
205	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
206	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
207	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
208	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
209	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
210	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
211	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
212	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
213	
214	\begin{lemma}
215	
216	
217	\leanok
218	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
219	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
221	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
222	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
223	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
224	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
225	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
226	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
227	  \(S_M\)-module.
228	\end{lemma}
229	
230	\begin{proof}
231	  The canonical comparison map
232	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
233	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
234	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
235	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
236	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
237	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
238	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
239	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
240	  the conclusion.
241	\end{proof}
242	
243	\begin{lemma}
244	
245	
246	\leanok
247	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
248	  the relative dimension]
249	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
250	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
251	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
252	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
253	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
254	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
255	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
256	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
257	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
258	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
259	\end{lemma}
260	
261	\begin{proof}
262	  Mathlib's
263	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
264	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
265	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
266	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
267	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
268	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
269	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
270	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
271	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
272	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
273	  ranks equal \(n\), so
274	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
275	\end{proof}
276	
277	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
278	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
279	
280	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
281	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
282	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
283	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
284	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
285	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
286	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
287	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
288	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
289	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
290	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
291	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
292	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
293	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
294	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
295	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
296	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
297	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
298	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
299	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
300	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
301	named iter-199+ prover targets.
302	
303	\begin{lemma}
304	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
305	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
306	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
307	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
308	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
309	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
310	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
311	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
312	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
313	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
314	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
315	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
316	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
317	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
318	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
319	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
320	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
321	  \[
322	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
323	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
324	  \]
325	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
326	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
327	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
328	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
329	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
330	
331	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
332	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
333	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
334	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
335	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
336	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
337	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
338	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
339	\end{lemma}
340	
341	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "By Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-prime-polynomial-ring}
342	% we know that $r = \text{trdeg}_k\ \kappa(\mathfrak p)$ is equal to the
343	% dimension of $V(\mathfrak p)$. Pick any maximal chain of primes
344	% $\mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots \subset \mathfrak p_r$
345	% starting with $\mathfrak p$ in $S$. This has length $r$ by Lemma
346	% \ref{lemma-dimension-spell-it-out}. Let $\mathfrak q_j$, $j \in J$ be
347	% the minimal primes of $S$ which are contained in $\mathfrak p$. These
348	% correspond $1-1$ to minimal primes in $S_{\mathfrak p}$ via the rule
349	% $\mathfrak q_j \mapsto \mathfrak q_jS_{\mathfrak p}$. By Lemma
350	% \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-over-field} we know that
351	% $\dim_x(X)$ is equal to the maximum of the dimensions of the rings
352	% $S/\mathfrak q_j$. For each $j$ pick a maximal chain of primes
353	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
354	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p$. Then $\dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) =
355	% \max_{j \in J} s(j)$. Now, each chain
356	% $\mathfrak q_j \subset \mathfrak p'_1 \subset \ldots \subset
357	% \mathfrak p'_{s(j)} = \mathfrak p \subset \mathfrak p_1 \subset \ldots
358	% \subset \mathfrak p_r$ is a maximal chain in $S/\mathfrak q_j$, and by
359	% what was said before we have $\dim_x(X) = \max_{j \in J} r + s(j)$.
360	% The lemma follows."
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
363	  This is exactly Stacks tag~\texttt{00OE}
364	  (\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}); the proof is a
365	  citation of the dimension theory of finitely generated algebras over a
366	  field. By Noether normalisation the finite-type \(k\)-algebra \(S\) is
367	  finite over a polynomial subalgebra, so the going-up / catenary
368	  dimension theory of such algebras applies. Write
369	  \(r = \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); this transcendence
370	  degree is exactly the dimension of the closed subvariety
371	  \(\overline{\{x\}} = V(\mathfrak q)\). A maximal chain of primes
372	  through \(\mathfrak q\) therefore splits into a
373	  length-\(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) part below \(\mathfrak q\) (recording
374	  the local codimension, i.e.\ the height of \(\mathfrak q\) in the
375	  relevant irreducible component) and a length-\(r\) part above it
376	  (recording the dimension of \(\overline{\{x\}}\)). Taking the maximum
377	  over the minimal primes contained in \(\mathfrak q\) yields
378	  \[
379	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + r
380	    = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q),
381	  \]
382	  which is the asserted formula.
383	
384	  For the geometric specialisation matching the Lean target, take
385	  \(k = \bar k\) algebraically closed and \(S\) standard smooth of
386	  relative dimension \(n\), so that \(\dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\) via
387	  \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (the free
388	  K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}\) has rank \(n\), which
389	  pins the relative dimension). The formula then gives
390	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak
391	  q)\). At a closed point \(\mathfrak q\) is maximal, so by the
392	  Nullstellensatz \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\) and
393	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\); hence
394	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S) = n\).
395	\end{proof}
396	
397	\begin{lemma}
398	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
399	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
400	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
401	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
402	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
403	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
404	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
405	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
406	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
407	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
408	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
409	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
410	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
411	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
412	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
413	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
414	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
415	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
416	  % $S'$-modules
417	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
418	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
419	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
420	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
421	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
422	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
423	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
424	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
425	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
426	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
427	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
428	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
429	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
430	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
431	  \[
432	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
433	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
434	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
435	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
436	  \]
437	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
438	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
439	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
440	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
441	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
442	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
443	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
444	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
445	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
446	  \[
447	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
448	  \]
449	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
450	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
451	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
452	\end{lemma}
453	
454	\begin{lemma}
455	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
456	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
457	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
458	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
459	  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
460	  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
461	  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
462	  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
463	  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
464	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
465	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
466	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
467	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
468	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
469	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
470	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
471	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
472	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
473	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
474	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
475	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
476	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
477	  dimension equals \(1\).
478	\end{lemma}
479	
480	\begin{proof}
481	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
482	  \begin{enumerate}
483	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
484	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
485	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
486	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
487	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
488	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
489	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
490	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
491	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
492	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
493	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
494	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
495	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
496	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
497	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
498	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
499	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
500	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
501	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
502	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
503	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
504	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
505	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
506	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
507	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
508	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
509	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
510	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
511	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
512	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
513	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
514	      the conclusion.
515	  \end{enumerate}
516	\end{proof}

636	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
637	\textit{Source: \texttt{coe-stacks00oe} mathlib-analogist
638	cross-domain-inspiration recipe (iter-200, persistent
639	\texttt{analogies/coe-stacks00oe.md}); 7 axiom-clean

696	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
697	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
698	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
699	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
700	
701	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
702	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
703	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
704	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
705	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
706	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
707	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's
708	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\));
709	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
710	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
711	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
712	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
713	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
714	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
715	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
716	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
717	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
718	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.

17	This chapter introduces the project's named declaration for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-\(0\) Albanese-witness argument: rigidity of morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\) to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, \textbf{over an algebraically closed base field \(\bar k\) of characteristic zero} (the alg-closed pivot of \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot" below; descent of the constancy conclusion to a general base field \(k\) is a separate downstream step, hosted in the genus-\(0\) witness chapter). The classical statement (Mumford, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Chapter~II~\S4) is
18	
19	\begin{quote}
20	  \emph{Let \(A\) be an abelian variety of positive dimension over an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\). Then every morphism \(f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) is constant.}
21	\end{quote}
22	
23	This is the keystone classical input for the C.2.d sub-step of Jacobian.tex \S C.2: the genus-\(0\) universal-property step of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} that concludes the universal-pointed-morphism factorisation by ruling out positive-dimensional images of \(f \colon C \to A\) when \(C\) has genus \(0\) and a \(k\)-rational marked point.
24	
25	\paragraph{Disposition (iter-155): \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} is a gated NAMED GAP.} The named declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar} is landed with a \texttt{sorry} body and is held as a \textbf{named gap} in the same family as \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (Jacobian.tex) and the deferred Serre-duality / Riemann--Roch stack --- honestly disclosed, not on a prover lane. The substantive content (the C.2.d sub-step below, ruling out a \(1\)-dimensional image of \(f\)) reduces to the vanishing of the cotangent pull-back \(\mathrm df \colon f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{C/\bar k}\), and a decisive iter-155 \texttt{mathlib-analogist} consult (\texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}) established that \textbf{producing \(\mathrm df = 0\) is irreducibly a global-sections fact}, gated on two genuinely-absent inputs:
26	
27	\begin{itemize}
28	  \item \textbf{(gap i) Global cotangent-triviality of \(A\):} \(\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_A^{\oplus g}\), generated by translation-invariant differentials. The presheaf globalisation that would build this (\texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) was \textbf{excised from the Lean tree at iter-145}; \texttt{GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} \(+\) \texttt{shearMulRight} survive only as a \emph{design template}. Estimated \(\sim 800\)--\(1500\) LOC. Mathlib's only ``group cotangent is trivial'' idiom is the smooth-manifold \texttt{GroupLieAlgebra} stack, which does not port to schemes.
29	  \item \textbf{(gap ii) Serre duality \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\)} for the genus-\(0\) curve. Given the global trivialisation of gap (i), \(\mathrm df \in H^0\bigl(C, \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr) \cong \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}\), so \(\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\). Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf and no Riemann--Roch; the project's \texttt{Cohomology/*} stack computes \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\) (genus) but has \textbf{no \(H^0\)-vanishing lemma and no genus\(\leftrightarrow\Omega\) bridge}. The genus-\(0\) hypothesis \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\) does \emph{not} yield \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\) without the duality. Estimated \(\sim 3000\)--\(8000\) LOC.
30	\end{itemize}

32	\noindent The chart-algebra envelope of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} (\cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero} (KDM), \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}, and the delegate \cref{lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart}), which \emph{is} closed and axiom-clean, supplies only the \textbf{converse} half of the bridge: ``if \(\mathrm df = 0\) then \(f\) factors through \(\Spec \bar k\) (is constant)''. It \emph{cannot} produce \(\mathrm df = 0\): on each affine chart \(V \subseteq C\), \(\Omega_{C/\bar k}(V)\) is free of rank \(1\) (nonzero), so a chart-by-chart computation never detects the vanishing --- the vanishing materialises only on global sections. Earlier framings in this chapter (the piece-(iv) ``DEFERRED, not on the C.2.d critical path'' disposition and the ``No-Serre-duality layer'') asserted that the chart-algebra route \emph{avoids} Serre duality; \textbf{that is false}, and is corrected throughout \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}: \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\) is \emph{on} the critical path for producing \(\mathrm df = 0\), not avoided.

40	\paragraph{Iter-152 alg-closed pivot.} The project committed an architectural pivot: rigidity is proved \textbf{only over an algebraically closed base field \(\bar k\) of characteristic zero}, and the constancy conclusion descends to a general base field \(k\) downstream. The reason is that the chart-algebra core lemma \cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero} (KDM) is mathematically \emph{false} as a bare \(B\)-only algebra lemma: a prover lane exhibited two counterexamples that satisfy every hypothesis yet have \(\ker D \supsetneq \operatorname{range}(\mathtt{algebraMap})\) --- namely \(B = k \times k\) (finite \'etale over \(k\)) and \(B = \mathbb Q(\sqrt 2)\) over \(\mathbb Q\). The missing content is precisely ``\(k\) algebraically closed in \(B\)''. Adding the \emph{joint} hypotheses \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} \(+\) \texttt{[IsDomain B]} makes it true: \texttt{[IsDomain B]} kills the \(B = k \times k\) counterexample and \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} kills the \(\mathbb Q(\sqrt 2)/\mathbb Q\) counterexample. (Neither hypothesis suffices alone; the iter-151 note that ``\texttt{[IsDomain B]} alone fails'' considered the hypotheses only one-at-a-time.) Over an algebraically closed base the \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field} constants lemma also collapses to a one-liner via \texttt{IsAlgClosed.algebraMap\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}, and the entire (S3.\textsc{sep}/\textsc{pi}) base-change-to-\(\bar k\) chain is descoped from the M2.a critical path.

42	\textbf{The Lean declaration's signature now carries \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} \(+\) \texttt{[CharZero kbar]}} (matching the theorem's existing docstring, which already described \(\bar k\) as algebraically closed --- the hypothesis was simply absent from the formal statement). Prior strategy iterations had instead routed through base-change to \(\bar k\) followed by Galois descent of morphism equality (M2.c, \(\sim 4\)--\(8\) iter / \(300\)--\(500\) LOC); the iter-127 framing then claimed all three pieces of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile build over an \emph{arbitrary} base field, dropping descent entirely. The alg-closed pivot supersedes that framing for the rigidity theorem itself: rigidity is established over \(\bar k\), and the descent of the constancy conclusion to a general \(k\) is a short downstream consequence of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective} (faithfully-flat covers are epimorphisms of schemes), with the detailed descent living in the genus-\(0\) witness chapter. The group-scheme cotangent-triviality pieces (i)/(iii) below remain genuinely intrinsic to the group scheme and are stated over a general base where that is sound; only the curve-side \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constancy chain (the KDM/constants core) requires the alg-closed base.

56	  \label{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar}
58	  \leanok
59	  % SOURCE: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch. II §4 (verbatim text not yet
60	  % retrieved — paywalled, no open copy in references/). No SOURCE QUOTE

63	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, II §4.}
64	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field of characteristic zero (Lean: \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} \(+\) \texttt{[CharZero kbar]}; in the use case, the algebraic closure of a base field \(k\), with descent of the conclusion to \(k\) handled downstream in the genus-\(0\) witness chapter). These two hypotheses match the theorem's existing docstring and are required by the curve-side \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constancy chain (\cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero}, \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}); see \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot". Let \(C : \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(\genus(C) = 0\) (i.e.\ \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\)). Let \(A : \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over \(\bar k\), with identity element \(\eta_A \in A(\bar k)\). Let
65	  \[
66	    f \colon C \longrightarrow A
67	  \]
68	  be a morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes over \(\Spec \bar k\), and let \(p \in C(\bar k)\) be a \(\bar k\)-rational point such that \(f(p) = \eta_A\). Then \(f\) equals the constant morphism at \(\eta_A\):
69	  \[
70	    f \;=\; \bigl(C \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A\bigr).
71	  \]
72	
73	  \emph{Encoding note (iter-126).} The Lean signature uses the abstract characterization \(\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 1\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \mathtt{IsProper}\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\ C.\mathtt{hom} + \genus(C) = 0\) instead of a literal \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) scheme. The two encodings are equivalent over \(\bar k\) once the curve has a \(\bar k\)-rational point (Brauer--Severi triviality + Riemann--Roch on \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)); the abstract form is used because Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} does not ship a packaged \(\mathtt{ProjectiveSpace}\,n\,S\) as a \(\mathtt{Scheme.Over}\,S\), and the C.2.b–C.2.d proof decomposition below works for any smooth proper geometrically integral genus-0 curve over \(\bar k\) without explicit \(\mathbb P^1\) API.
74	\end{theorem}

95	  \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_proof_decomposition} (steps C.2.b–C.2.e) on top of
96	  the shared cotangent-vanishing pile of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}
97	  (pieces (i)–(iv)). The keystone step C.2.d is gated on a route choice (route
98	  (a) cotangent-bundle vs.\ route (b) dual-abelian-variety); we present the
99	  argument conditionally on whichever sub-lemmas the chapter names, so the
100	  closing recipe is a finite assembly of in-chapter (and cross-chapter)
101	  declarations.
102	
103	  \emph{Reduction to the rigidity lemma (C.2.b).} Let
104	  \(c \colon C \to A\) denote the constant morphism at \(\eta_A\), i.e.\ the
105	  composite \(C \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\eta_A} A\). To prove \(f = c\)
106	  it suffices, by the project's rigidity lemma
107	  \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}),
108	  to exhibit a non-empty open \(U \subseteq C\) on which
109	  \(U.\iota \circ f.\mathrm{left} = U.\iota \circ c.\mathrm{left}\) as scheme
110	  morphisms. The hypotheses of that lemma are met by the standing data:
111	  \(C\) is smooth over \(\bar k\), hence reduced, and geometrically
112	  irreducible; \(A\) is proper over \(\bar k\), hence separated. Thus once the
113	  set-level agreement is in hand on a non-empty open, the scheme-morphism
114	  equality \(f = c\) follows globally (this is the C.2.e promotion, integrated
115	  into the application of \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}).
116	
117	  \emph{Image-dimension dichotomy (C.2.c).} If \(g = 0\) then
118	  \(A = \Spec \bar k\) and the conclusion is vacuous
119	  (\cref{rem:rigidity_over_kbar_dim_dichotomy}); so assume \(g \geq 1\). The
120	  morphism \(f\) goes from the proper, geometrically integral, \(1\)-dimensional
121	  \(\bar k\)-scheme \(C\) to the smooth proper \(g\)-dimensional \(\bar k\)-scheme
122	  \(A\); its set-theoretic image \(f(C) \subseteq A\) is an irreducible closed
123	  subset (continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper, hence closed,
124	  map). The image therefore has dimension \(0\) or \(1\):
125	  \begin{itemize}
126	    \item If \(\dim f(C) = 0\), the image is a single point, which the pointed
127	      hypothesis \(f(p) = \eta_A\) pins to \(\{\eta_A\}\). Then \(f\) is constant
128	      on underlying topological points, and the set-level equality
129	      \(f = c\) on \(C\) is promoted to scheme-morphism equality via
130	      \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} as in C.2.b/C.2.e.
131	    \item If \(\dim f(C) = 1\), the image is an irreducible rational curve in
132	      \(A\). This case is excluded by the keystone C.2.d below.
133	  \end{itemize}
134	
135	  \emph{Keystone: no rational curves on a positive-dimensional abelian variety
136	  (C.2.d).} It remains to rule out a \(1\)-dimensional image. By Mumford's
137	  classical fact (\textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, II §4}), a proper
138	  rational curve on an abelian variety is constant; we reduce this to the
139	  vanishing of the cotangent pull-back
140	  \(\mathrm df \colon f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{C/\bar k}\). Producing
141	  \(\mathrm df = 0\) is a \emph{global-sections} fact, assembled as follows.
142	  \begin{itemize}
143	    \item \emph{Group-cotangent triviality (piece (i)).} The relative cotangent
144	      \(\Omega_{A/\bar k}\) of the group scheme \(A\) is free of rank \(g\): by
145	      \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} it is the pull-back along \(A \to \Spec \bar k\)
146	      of the cotangent space at the identity \(\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_A^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}\)
147	      (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}), a finitely generated free
148	      \(\bar k\)-module, and by \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_rank_eq_dim} (resting on
149	      the rank computation \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}) its rank equals
150	      \(g\). Pulling back along \(f\) gives a global trivialisation
151	      \(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}\), under which
152	      \(\mathrm df\) is a \(g\)-tuple of global sections of \(\Omega_{C/\bar k}\),
153	      i.e.\ \(\mathrm df \in \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}\).
154	    \item \emph{Genus-\(0\) cohomological vanishing (piece (iv)).} Consequently
155	      \(\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\). The right-hand
156	      vanishing is the genus-\(0\) instance of the curve-side cotangent
157	      cohomology; it is established by a two-chart \v Cech / Mayer--Vietoris
158	      computation directly on \(\Omega_{C/\bar k}^{\oplus g}\) via
159	      \cref{thm:Scheme_AffineCoverMVSquare_HModule_prime_sequence_exact} (the
160	      same idiom the project uses for \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0\) on a genus-\(0\)
161	      curve), rather than by a packaged Serre-duality theorem. \emph{This step
162	      is gated on the route choice:} route (a) builds pieces (i)\(+\)(ii)
163	      directly to obtain the trivialisation and the vanishing; route (b) obtains
164	      the same conclusion through the dual abelian variety via
165	      \(\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0\). The argument below is stated
166	      conditionally on whichever route supplies \(\mathrm df = 0\); neither route
167	      is committed here.
168	    \item \emph{Converse: \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f\) constant (piece (ii)).}
169	      Given \(\mathrm df = 0\), the chart-algebra envelope furnishes constancy.
170	      On each affine chart \(V = \Spec R \subseteq C\) lying over a chart
171	      \(W = \Spec B \subseteq A\), the per-chart helper
172	      \cref{lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart} shows
173	      that the chart map \(f^{\sharp} \colon B \to R\) factors as
174	      \(B \to \bar k \to R\): it delegates the ring-side kernel extraction to
175	      the K\"ahler core \cref{lem:KaehlerDifferential_mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero}
176	      (KDM) and the integral-closure of the constants to
177	      \cref{lem:constants_integral_over_base_field}. The hypotheses
178	      \texttt{[CharZero kbar]} and \texttt{[IsAlgClosed kbar]} carried by the
179	      theorem are exactly what these two helpers require: characteristic zero
180	      makes the Frobenius-iteration layer (piece (iii)) vacuous, so the
181	      derivation-kernel argument applies directly, and algebraic closedness
182	      collapses the constants lemma to the bijectivity of
183	      \(\mathtt{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,\bar k(b)\). Assembling the chart
184	      factorisations by sheaf compatibility and globalising via
185	      \cref{lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero}, the morphism \(f\) factors
186	      through \(\Spec \bar k\); hence \(f\) is constant, contradicting
187	      \(\dim f(C) = 1\). This rules out the \(1\)-dimensional image.
188	  \end{itemize}
189	
190	  \emph{Conclusion (C.2.b/C.2.e).} The dichotomy C.2.c thus collapses to the
191	  single point case: \(f(C) = \{\eta_A\}\). Taking \(U := C\), the resulting
192	  set-level agreement \(f = c\) is promoted to scheme-morphism equality by
193	  \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (reduced source \(C\), separated target
194	  \(A\)). Therefore
195	  \[
196	    f \;=\; \bigl(C \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A\bigr),
197	  \]
198	  as claimed.
199	\end{proof}
200	
201	\begin{remark}
202	  \label{rem:rigidity_over_kbar_dim_dichotomy}
203	  
204	  The conclusion is vacuously true when \(A\) is zero-dimensional: in that case \(A = \Spec \bar k\) and every morphism \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) factors through \(\Spec \bar k\) trivially. The substantive content of the theorem is the positive-dimensional case, which decomposes into the steps of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_proof_decomposition}.
205	\end{remark}
206	
207	\section{Proof decomposition}
208	\label{sec:RigidityKbar_proof_decomposition}
209	
210	The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} decomposes as follows. We reuse the sub-step labelling of Jacobian.tex \S C.2 so cross-references between the two chapters remain unambiguous. \textbf{This decomposition is the eventual closing recipe for a gated named gap} (see the iter-155 disposition in the chapter introduction): the substantive step C.2.d reduces to producing \(\mathrm df = 0\), which is gated on gaps (i)\(+\)(ii) [route (a)] or on the dual-AV keystone [route (b)]. The steps below stay inert --- the Lean body is a \texttt{sorry} --- until a route is chosen and built; none names a present, assembled Mathlib infrastructure for the image-dimension and \(\mathrm df = 0\) content.
211	
212	\begin{itemize}
213	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.b)}] \emph{Reduction to the project's rigidity lemma.} Let \(c \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) denote the constant morphism at \(\eta_A\). The conclusion \(f = c\) follows from \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (i.e.\ \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, after the iter-125 refactor) applied to \(g_1 := f\), \(g_2 := c\), \(X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), \(Y := A\), once we produce a non-empty open \(U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) on which \(U.\iota \circ f.\mathrm{left} = U.\iota \circ c.\mathrm{left}\) as scheme morphisms. We will take \(U := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\). The refactored rigidity lemma's hypotheses are trivially satisfied: \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is smooth (hence reduced) and geometrically irreducible over \(\bar k\), and \(A\) is proper (hence separated) over \(\bar k\).
214	
215	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.c)}] \emph{Image dimension argument.} The morphism \(f\) goes from a proper geometrically integral \(\bar k\)-scheme of dimension \(1\) to a smooth proper \(\bar k\)-scheme of dimension \(g := \dim_{\bar k} A\). The set-theoretic image \(f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) \subseteq A\) is an irreducible closed subset of \(A\) (continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper map). \emph{Gating note (iter-155): this dichotomy names no present, assembled Mathlib infrastructure} --- the ``image of a proper map is closed and irreducible'' fact and a usable notion of scheme/image dimension are not packaged for this setting in snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; C.2.c is part of the gated assembly, to be detailed once a route (a)/(b) is chosen. Dichotomy:
216	  \begin{itemize}
217	    \item If \(g = 0\): \(A = \Spec \bar k\) and the conclusion is vacuous (\cref{rem:rigidity_over_kbar_dim_dichotomy}).
218	    \item If \(g \geq 1\) and the image is a single point: the image must be \(\{\eta_A\}\) (the pointed hypothesis \(f(p) = \eta_A\) pins it). Then \(f\) is constant on underlying topological points, and the conclusion follows by promoting set-level equality to scheme-morphism equality (sub-step C.2.e of Jacobian.tex, applied via \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}).
219	    \item If \(g \geq 1\) and the image is one-dimensional: the image is an irreducible rational curve in \(A\). This case is ruled out by the keystone classical input (C.2.d below).
220	  \end{itemize}
221	
222	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.d)}] \emph{Keystone: proper rational curves on an abelian variety are constant --- the gated content.} The remaining case --- the image of \(f\) has dimension exactly \(1\) --- is ruled out by Mumford's classical fact stated in the chapter introduction, and is the load-bearing reduction of the whole theorem. It reduces to the vanishing \(\mathrm df = 0\) of the cotangent pull-back \(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{C/\bar k}\). Per the iter-155 disposition (chapter introduction) and \texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}, producing \(\mathrm df = 0\) is a \textbf{global-sections} fact: with the global trivialisation \(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}\) (gap i), \(\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\) (gap ii, Serre duality on the genus-\(0\) curve). The two standard proofs sketched in Jacobian.tex \S C.2.d furnish the two gated closure routes that the project commits to neither of yet: \textbf{route (a)} = the cotangent-bundle route, building gaps (i)\(+\)(ii) directly (triviality of \(\Omega_{A/\bar k}\) + \(H^0(C,\Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\)); \textbf{route (b)} = the dual-abelian-variety route via \(\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0\), which may amortise the \(\Pic^0\) engine that the positive-genus arm of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} needs regardless. The chart-algebra envelope of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} supplies only the converse ``\(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constant''; it does \emph{not} produce \(\mathrm df = 0\), and (contrary to earlier framing) does not avoid the Serre-duality input.
223	
224	  \item[\textbf{(C.2.e)}] \emph{Promotion of set-level equality to scheme-morphism equality.} Standard reduced-source / separated-target argument, integrated into the application of \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} in C.2.b.
225	\end{itemize}
226	
227	\section{The shared cotangent-vanishing Mathlib pile}
228	\label{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}
229	
230	The C.2.d keystone above (\emph{morphisms from \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) to a positive-dimensional abelian variety over \(\bar k\) are constant}) and the genus-\(0\) identification alternative of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} (M2.d-alt of STRATEGY.md, which sidesteps Riemann--Roch by running the cotangent-vanishing argument directly) depend on the same Mathlib pile. STRATEGY.md \S M2.d-alt names this the \emph{shared cotangent-vanishing pile}. The iter-126 mathlib-analogist consult (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md}) decomposed the pile into the four pieces below, revised the per-piece LOC budgets upward where the directive had under-scoped, and made the characteristic-\(p\) option pick:
231	
232	\begin{itemize}
233	  \item[(i)] \emph{Group-scheme cotangent triviality.} The relative cotangent presheaf \(\Omega_{G/k}\) of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme \(G\) over a base field \(k\) (this piece is genuinely intrinsic to the group scheme and needs no algebraic-closure hypothesis, even under the iter-152 alg-closed pivot of \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot"; only the curve-side \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constancy chain requires \(\bar k\) algebraically closed) is free of rank \(\dim G\). Per the iter-126 analogist's naming-idiom alignment (Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227} has \emph{no} \texttt{AbelianVariety} file; the \texttt{GrpObj} namespace per Yang+Merten 2026 is the canonical home), target names are \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.omega\_free} and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.omega\_rank\_eq\_dim}. A prover-ready three-step sub-lemma decomposition (i.a)\,\(\to\)\,(i.b)\,\(\to\)\,(i.c) for the iter-128+ build lane is given in \cref{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition}. Estimated build: \textbf{800--1500 LOC} (iter-126 analogist's correction over an earlier 200--400 LOC framing; the triviality is the chain Lie-algebra-of-\texttt{GrpObj} + shear-iso globalisation + relative-cotangent-presheaf trivialisation on top of the project's existing \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}, inheriting the presheaf-vs-sheaf bridge cost from \texttt{analogies/cotangent-presheaf-design.md}).
234	
235	  \item[(ii)] \emph{Scheme-level \(\mathrm d f = 0\) implies \(f\) factors through \(\Spec k\).} The companion algebraic fact: if a morphism \(f \colon X \to Y\) between smooth proper \(k\)-schemes has \(\mathrm d f \colon f^* \Omega_{Y/k} \to \Omega_{X/k}\) the zero map (e.g.\ because \(\Omega_{Y/k}\) is trivial and \(H^0(X, \Omega_{X/k}) = 0\)), then \(f\) factors through the constant morphism. In characteristic \(0\) this is the standard differential-vanishing-implies-constancy argument. In characteristic \(p > 0\) it requires the Frobenius iteration of piece (iii). Per the analogist's recommendation: the ring-level half aligns with Mathlib's \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Derivation.DifferentialRing}, lines 62--70; one Mathlib consumer at \texttt{FieldTheory.Differential.Liouville}); the scheme-level half is a NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL gap and the canonical name is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_diff\_zero} (following the iter-125-refactored \texttt{ext\_of\_eqOnOpen} idiom). Estimated build: \textbf{250--500 LOC}.
236	
237	  \item[(iii)] \emph{Characteristic-\(p\) handling --- \textbf{committed Option A: Frobenius iteration}.} Per the analogist's decisive pick (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md} \S "Option A wins"): the three options on the table are
238	  \begin{itemize}
239	    \item Frobenius iteration (replace \(f\) with \(f \circ F^n\) for the \(n\)-th iterate of Frobenius; descent of the conclusion uses smoothness of \(A\));
240	    \item Mumford's argument (Mumford, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Chapter~II~\S4: abelian varieties contain no rational curves in characteristic \(p\));
241	    \item Lifting to characteristic \(0\) via Witt vectors.
242	  \end{itemize}
243	  Options B (Mumford) and C (Witt) each carry standalone multi-thousand-LOC dependencies (Mumford requires the very Pic\(^0\) / cotangent machinery the project is trying to streamline; Witt-vector lifting requires a scheme-level Witt functor + smooth deformation + descent that Mathlib entirely lacks). Option A consumes Mathlib's first-class ring-level \texttt{frobenius} / \texttt{iterateFrobenius} from \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius} (lines 30--110+) and aligns with the project's existing scheme-level Frobenius design context (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean:48--55}); the scheme-level Frobenius lift remains a NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL gap, but Option A's net cost is dominated by adapting the ring-level idiom upward to schemes. Estimated build: \textbf{300--600 LOC}.
244	
245	  \item[(iv)] \emph{Serre duality on a smooth proper curve --- \textbf{DEFERRED named gap, ON the C.2.d critical path (iter-155 correction)}.} The honest-closure verdict (\texttt{analogies/serre-duality.md}, iter-110) is \textbf{3000--8000 LOC}. \textbf{Correction (iter-155).} Earlier text here claimed piece (iv) was used ``by the M2.d-alt genus-\(0\) identification alternative, NOT by C.2.d itself'' and that ``C.2.d / M2.a body closure does NOT depend on piece (iv)''. \emph{That is false.} The iter-155 \texttt{mathlib-analogist} consult (\texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}) established that the C.2.d keystone reduces to producing \(\mathrm df = 0\), which is a global-sections fact: with the gap-(i) trivialisation \(f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}\), one has \(\mathrm df \in \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}\), so \(\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\) --- which is exactly the genus-\(0\) instance of this Serre-duality piece (iv). Hence piece (iv) (or an equivalent that yields \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/\bar k}) = 0\)) is \textbf{on} the C.2.d critical path under the cotangent-bundle closure route (a); only the dual-AV route (b) can in principle bypass it, by amortising the \(\Pic^0\) engine. The chart-by-chart KDM stack does \emph{not} avoid this input (see the correction in \cref{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition}). Piece (iv) remains a deferred named gap; it is not opened on a prover lane until a closure route is committed.
246	\end{itemize}

248	\subsection{Piece (i): sub-lemma decomposition for iter-128+ build}
249	\label{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition}
250	
251	We decompose piece (i) above into three sub-steps (i.a)\,\(\to\)\,(i.b)\,\(\to\)\,(i.c) that are well-suited to staged Lean formalisation. Sub-step (i.a) defines the Lie algebra (as a \(k\)-vector space) of a group scheme at the identity and pins its rank; sub-step (i.b) globalises that fibre across \(G\) via a functorial shear isomorphism; sub-step (i.c) concludes the freeness and rank of the relative cotangent \(\Omega_{G/k}\).
252	
253	All three sub-steps of this group-scheme cotangent-triviality piece are stated over an arbitrary base field \(k\) (the cotangent triviality of \(G\) is intrinsic to the group scheme and does not need the alg-closed hypothesis; see \S "Iter-152 alg-closed pivot" in the chapter introduction, where the alg-closed base is required only for the curve-side \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constancy chain, not here); the prose carefully avoids any reliance on \(k\)-rational or \(\bar k\)-rational point lifting, working functorially throughout. Sub-step (i.a) is the natural first target for the iter-128+ prover lane: it has the smallest signature surface (a finite-rank free \(k\)-module attached to the identity section of \(G\)), and no scheme-level globalisation.
254	
255	\paragraph{Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot --- disposition (per \texttt{mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144} + STRATEGY.md \S "Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot" block).}
256	Effective iter-145+, the M2.body-pile commits to the \textbf{chart-algebra route}. Under this commitment:
257	\begin{itemize}
258	  \item Piece (i.b) Step 2 d\_app sub-sorry inside \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}'s proof, the IsIso theorem \cref{lem:GrpObj_basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso} (Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso}), and piece (i.b) Main \cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises} (Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) are \textbf{DESCOPED from critical-path}. \emph{Status correction (iter-155):} \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} was subsequently \textbf{EXCISED from the Lean tree at iter-145}; \texttt{GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} \(+\) \texttt{shearMulRight} survive only as a \emph{design template}, \textbf{not} a present declaration. This excised gap-(i) globalisation (\(\sim 800\)--\(1500\) LOC) is exactly the gap (i) that the keystone \(\mathrm df = 0\) (chapter introduction) is gated on; the blueprint blocks below for these declarations are therefore an off-path record of the bundled-route design, not a live in-tree obligation.
259	  \item Piece (i.c.1/i.c.2/i.c.3) (\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_free} + \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_rank_eq_dim} assembly) is \textbf{DESCOPED}. Chart-algebra routes the freeness and rank facts through chart-level \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} per-chart, not through a global sheaf-of-modules construction.
260	  \item Piece (iii) scheme-level absolute Frobenius PHANTOM (\textasciitilde\(800\)--\(1500\) LOC, \texttt{Scheme.absoluteFrobenius} ABSENT in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) is \textbf{DESCOPED}; chart-algebra uses ring-level \texttt{RingHom.iterateFrobenius\_comm} from \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius} per-chart, giving Frobenius iteration as a direct \texttt{RingHom}-commutation with no scheme-level lift.
261	  \item Piece (ii) is \textbf{INFLATED} to absorb the chart-algebra (\(\alpha\)) \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-from-affine-product helper and (\(\beta\)) per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation argument upstream; see the \S "Iter-144 chart-algebra envelope for piece (ii)" paragraph below for the updated envelope (\textasciitilde\(600\)--\(1050\) LOC total).
262	\end{itemize}
263	
264	Piece (i.a) --- \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} (\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}) and \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} (\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}), closed iter-128 \(\to\) iter-132 --- remains in-tree as standalone infrastructure. Chart-algebra does \emph{not} consume it on the rigidity critical path, but the cotangent-space-at-the-identity definition and its rank are independently useful downstream (Lie-algebra dual \(\mathfrak g\) on the tangent side, etc.).
265	
266	\paragraph{Iter-144 chart-algebra envelope for piece (ii) (per \texttt{mathlib-analogist-chart-algebra-iter144}).}
267	Piece (ii) (scheme-level \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f\) factors through \(\Spec k\), the C.2.d / M2.a body consumer of piece (i)'s cotangent-trivial output) is the load-bearing body under the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot. Total envelope \textasciitilde\(600\)--\(1050\) LOC, decomposed as:
268	\begin{itemize}
269	  \item Chart-algebra (\(\alpha\)) \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout}-from-affine-product helper: \textasciitilde\(80\)--\(150\) LOC. Constructs the \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout} instance on the chart-level ring extensions \(\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(W, \mathcal O_G)\) for affine charts \(V \to W\) in the product \(G \otimes G\). Built from \texttt{pullbackSpecIso} and the \texttt{\_hom\_fst} / \texttt{\_inv\_fst} simp companions in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullbacks}, chained with \texttt{CommRingCat.isPushout\_iff\_isPushout} and \texttt{isPullback\_SpecMap\_of\_isPushout} (cf.\ \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj} Route (a) item 1).
270	  \item Chart-algebra (\(\beta\)) per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation argument: \textasciitilde\(150\)--\(300\) LOC. The load-bearing core of M2.a's \(\mathrm df = 0\) derivation chain (per STRATEGY.md \S "Soundness rules: M2.a \(\mathrm df = 0\) derivation chain --- articulated (added iter-144)"). The three-layer chain is:
271	    \begin{enumerate}
272	      \item \textbf{Chart-local layer.} On each affine chart \(W = \Spec B \subseteq A.\mathrm{left}\) with affine \(V \subseteq C.\mathrm{left}\) such that \(f\) restricts to a ring map \(f^{\sharp} \colon B \to \Gamma(C, V)\), the differential \(\mathrm df = 0\) at the chart level means the composite \(\Omega_{B/k} \to \Omega_{\Gamma(C, V)/k}\) obtained from the universal Kähler derivation \(\mathrm d \colon B \to \Omega_{B/k}\) post-composed with \(f^{\sharp}\) vanishes. Combined with piece (i)'s chart-level free-of-rank-\(n\) presentation of \(\Omega_{B/k}\) (\texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential}), the kernel-extraction step gives \(f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}\) for every \(b \in B\), where \(p = \operatorname{char} k\) and \(e \geq 0\) is a chart-uniform Frobenius depth.
273	      \item \textbf{Char-\(p\) Frobenius-iteration layer.} The chart-local conclusion \(f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}\) is promoted to "for every \(b \in B\), there exists \(e\) with \(f^{\sharp}(b) = \mathrm{iterateFrobenius}_{p, e}(c_{b})\) for some \(c_{b} \in \Gamma(C, V)\)" via \texttt{RingHom.iterateFrobenius\_comm} (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius}, verified iter-144). The uniform-\(e\)-on-\(W\) glue extracts a globally-bounded Frobenius depth on \(C\) from the smoothness of \(A\) at the image of \(f\) (using \(A\)'s tangent-bundle triviality at each point in the image of \(f\) to control the depth uniformly across \(W\)). In characteristic \(0\) this layer is vacuous: \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f^{\sharp}(b) \in k \subseteq \Gamma(C, V)\) directly via the standard derivation-kernel argument (no Frobenius).
274	      \item \textbf{Constancy-from-\(\mathrm df = 0\) layer (the converse only).} \emph{Iter-155 correction.} This layer was previously titled the ``No-Serre-duality layer'' and claimed the chart-algebra (\(\beta\)) chain ``replaces'' the \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0\) route, ``eliminating piece (iv) as a critical-path dependency''. \textbf{That claim is false} (per \texttt{analogies/df-zero-production-iter155.md}) and is corrected here. What the chain actually delivers is the \emph{converse} implication: \textbf{given} the chart-uniform input \(f^{\sharp}(b) \in \Gamma(C, V)^{p^{e}}\) (which is the chart-level shadow of the hypothesis \(\mathrm df = 0\)), the conclusion ``\(f\) factors through \(\Spec k\)'' follows from the integrally-closed-constants helper (next item) and the scheme-level lift via \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen} (final item). It does \emph{not} produce the hypothesis \(\mathrm df = 0\), and it does not avoid the \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0\) input: that input is exactly what is needed to \emph{establish} \(\mathrm df = 0\) in the first place. Concretely, \(\mathrm df = 0\) is a global-sections fact --- with the gap-(i) trivialisation \(f^{*}\Omega_{A/k} \cong \mathcal O_C^{\oplus g}\), \(\mathrm df \in \bigl(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k})\bigr)^{\oplus g}\), so \(\mathrm df = 0 \iff H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0\) --- and the chart-by-chart machinery cannot detect it because \(\Omega_{C/k}(V)\) is free of rank \(1\) (nonzero) on every affine chart \(V\). Hence piece (iv) (or an equivalent yielding \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0\)) is \textbf{on} the critical path for the keystone, not eliminated by this chain.
275	    \end{enumerate}
276	  \item Algebra-level core \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.mem\_range\_algebraMap\_of\_D\_eq\_zero}: \textasciitilde\(200\)--\(350\) LOC. Mathlib-shaped helper for the ring-level half of \(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow f^{\sharp}\) factors through \(k\), packaged as the statement "for \(D \colon R \to M\) the universal Kähler derivation with \(D \circ f^{\sharp} = 0\), the image of \(f^{\sharp}\) lies in the integral closure of the image of the base ring". This is the chart-uniform core consumed by layer (1) of the \(\beta\)-chain.
277	  \item Integrally-closed-constants helper: \textasciitilde\(50\)--\(100\) LOC. The wrapper around "constants in \(C\) are integral over \(k\) for \(C\) smooth proper geometrically irreducible", used to promote the chart-uniform Frobenius-depth conclusion to a global \(\Spec k\)-factorisation.
278	  \item Scheme-level lift via \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}: \textasciitilde\(100\)--\(150\) LOC. Closes the argument by exhibiting a non-empty open \(U \subseteq C\) on which \(f|_{U}\) equals the constant morphism at the image of \(f\)'s chart-uniform output, then applies the iter-125-refactored \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}) to globalise.
279	\end{itemize}
280	
281	The chart-algebra route replaces the bundled scheme-level absolute-Frobenius construction (piece (iii) PHANTOM) with chart-uniform ring-level idioms already in Mathlib snapshot \texttt{b80f227}. \textbf{It does not, however, replace the Serre-duality cohomology vanishing (piece (iv))} --- the iter-155 correction above and in \cref{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition} retracts that earlier claim. The chart-algebra (\(\beta\)) chain is the \emph{converse} ``\(\mathrm df = 0 \Rightarrow\) constant''; producing \(\mathrm df = 0\) remains a global-sections fact gated on \(H^0(C, \Omega_{C/k}) = 0\) (piece (iv)) plus the gap-(i) trivialisation. The Lean target name for the scheme-level converse half is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_diff\_zero} (per the iter-126 analogist's idiom alignment); see \cref{lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero} for the present thin-wrapper Lean decl versus the gated substantive \(\mathrm df = \mathrm dg\) refinement.
282	
283	\paragraph{Iter-128 / iter-129 prover lane re-scoping (per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128} + iter-129 rename).} Sub-step (i.a) was originally a 2-target pair and is now (post-iter-129) a 3-target trio: \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} (the \emph{definition} of \(\mathfrak g^\vee\), plus the finitely-generated-free claim — iter-128 lane, body landed), \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} (the \emph{bridge} to the local-ring cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}\) — iter-130+ build target), and \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} (the \emph{rank-equals-relative-dimension} claim — iter-130+ build target). The iter-128 prover lane attacks ONLY the first: it scaffolds the Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} (renamed iter-129 from the placeholder \texttt{lieAlgebra}; signature relaxed to a free \texttt{\{n : \mathbb N\} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} binder so downstream consumers of arbitrary relative dimension can instantiate it) and closes its body. The bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} is vestigial on the live path under Replacement (B) (see \S Q4 collapse footer at the rank-lemma proof's end) and remains deferred indefinitely; the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} is the iter-132 prover-lane target (closure path: Step 1 chart-side Kähler rank from \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}'s existential + Step 2 base-change to \(k\) via \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange}, both detailed in that lemma's proof body). Rationale: (i) bundling definition + bridge + rank into one prover-iter task is borderline-too-ambitious per \texttt{progress-critic-iter128}; (ii) the rank-lemma RHS Lean encoding needs blueprint pinning iter-129 before being prover-ready (it must read \texttt{Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G) = n} parameterised by an explicit \texttt{[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} instance; the bare \(\dim G\) in the prose does not type-check directly because Mathlib has no \(\dim G\) function on \texttt{Over (Spec k)}, per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128} CHALLENGE on (i.a) signature ambiguity).

285	Throughout this subsection, \(k\) is an arbitrary field and \(G : \Over\,(\Spec k)\) is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over \(k\), with identity section \(\eta_G : \mathbf{1} \to G\).
286	
287	\begin{lemma}\leanok
288	[(i.a) Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme]
289	  \label{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
290	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
291	  % Lean signature stub (pinned post-iter-129 fixup; relaxed from the
292	  % iter-128 hardcoded rel-dim-1 form so the same declaration serves
293	  % consumers of arbitrary relative dimension, e.g. \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
294	  % applied to an abelian variety of dimension \(g\)):
295	  %   noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
296	  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
297	  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] : ModuleCat k
298	  The pullback along the identity section \(\eta_G\) of the relative cotangent presheaf,
299	  \[
300	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;:=\; \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
301	  \]
302	  is a finitely generated free \(k\)-module. We call \(\mathfrak g^{\vee}\) the \emph{cotangent space of \(G\) at the identity}. The \emph{Lie algebra of \(G\)} as a \(k\)-vector space is recovered as the \(k\)-linear dual \(\mathfrak g := \mathfrak g^{\vee\vee} = \mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k)\) (the bracket structure is not used in the rigidity argument and is not packaged here).
303	
304	  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-129 dualisation pinning).} The Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} returns a \texttt{ModuleCat k} which is the \emph{un-dualised} cotangent \(\mathfrak g^{\vee}\) — the direct output of the pullback-along-section construction described in the proof, with no internal \texttt{Module.Dual} hop. Downstream consumers that need the tangent \(\mathfrak g\) take \texttt{Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)} externally. This convention pins what was left open in the iter-128 encoding note: the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the finite-rank-free claim is consumed), so the un-dualised form is preferred as it matches the body construction without an extra contravariant hop. The declaration embeds into Mathlib's \texttt{ModuleCat} infrastructure and supplies the \texttt{Module.Free k} / \texttt{Module.Finite k} typeclasses needed for the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} via the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}.
305	\end{lemma}
306	
307	\begin{proof}
308	
309	\leanok
310	  The identity section \(\eta_G\) is a canonical \(k\)-rational point of \(G\) supplied by the \(\GrpObj\) structure. The iter-131 Lean construction (Replacement (B), pure-term \texttt{noncomputable def}) realises \(\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}\) as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart \(V \subseteq G\) around the image of the identity section \(\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})\), with affine base chart \(U \subseteq \Spec k\) and the structural ring map \(\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)\) exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} \(n\) (and consequently \(\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}\) is a free \(\Gamma(G, V)\)-module of rank \(n\) by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to \(k\) of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map \(\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k\) obtained by restricting the identity section \(\eta_G\) to \(V\) and composing with the canonical isomorphism \(\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k\):
311	  \[
312	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\;:=\;\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,V)}\, \Omega_{\Gamma(G,\,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}.
313	  \]
314	  This is a finitely generated free \(k\)-module of rank \(n\) by base-change of a free module along \(\psi_V\) (the rank is preserved; see \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).
315	
316	  \emph{Caveat on canonicity.} The construction depends on a chart choice \(V\) extracted via a \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain from the existential statement of \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; it is therefore not canonically attached to \(G\) in the strictest sense. (The iter-131 fix-up refactored what was previously a top-level \texttt{Classical.choice}-wrapper into a pure-term \texttt{let}-chain of \texttt{Classical.choose} / \texttt{Classical.choose\_spec} accessors, exposing the outer head symbol \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars\,\(\psi_V\).hom).obj\,(ModuleCat.of\,\(\Gamma\)(G, V)\,\(\Omega\)[\(\Gamma\)(G, V)\,/\,\(\Gamma\)(Spec k, U)])} to downstream consumers; see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the Lean encoding details, and the companion \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace_extendScalars_witness} for the structural-shape rewrite handle.) For the live consumer (rigidity over \(k\), via \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}), the existence of a rank-\(n\) free \(k\)-module is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent \(\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}\) is available as a future bridge (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below, currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}) at additional cost; the project defers that bridge until a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires canonicity.
317	\end{proof}
